author: devakīnandana⋅dāsa
verses:
gajendragamane nitāi calaye manthare
jā’re dekhe tā’re bhāsāya premera pāthāre

patita durgata pāpīra ghare ghare giyā
brahmāra durlabha prema dicchena jāciyā

je nā laya, tā’re kaya dante tṛṇa dhari’
‘āmāre kiniyā loo, bala gaura⋅hari

to’ sabāra lāgiyā kṛṣṇera avatāra
śuna bhāi, gaurāṅga-sundara nadīyāra

je pa̐hu gokulapure nandera kumāra
to’ sabāra lāgi’ ebe kaila avatāra’
śuniyā kā̐daye pāpī caraṇe dhariyā
pulake pūrala aṅga gara gara hiyā

tā’re kole kari’ nitāi jāi ānaṭhāma
hena-mate preme bhāsāola pura⋅grāma

devakīnandana bole mui abhāgiyā
ḍubilu̐ viṣaya⋅kūpe nitāi nā bhajiyā
en_translation:
Lord Nityānanda walks slowly and majestically like a lordly elephant. Whomever He sees, He immerses them in the ocean of divine love.

Going from house to house of the fallen, the distressed, and the sinful, He begs them to accept the divine love that is rare even for Lord Brahmā.

To those who do not accept it, He says, holding a straw between His teeth, "Please purchase Me as your servant by chanting the name of Gaura⋅hari!"

"For the sake of all of you, Lord Kṛṣṇa has incarnated. Listen, O brother! He is Gaurāṅgasundara of Nadiyā."

"That Lord, who was the son of Nanda Mahārāja in Gokula, has now incarnated for all of you." Hearing this, the sinners cry, holding His feet, their bodies filled with ecstasy and their hearts overwhelmed.

Embracing them, Lord Nityānanda goes to another place. In this way, He inundated every town and village with divine love.

Devakīnandana Dāsa says: "I am so unfortunate. I have drowned in the well of material sense gratification, not having worshipped Lord Nityānanda."
